beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Nina draws on a classic agency lineage: Schwab, Publicis, McCann, and Saatchi & Saatchi. Wanting to see if it worked, the author fed it a real brand idea to test. Nina caught something during the intake stage that others would likely miss. She treated that caught detail as the entire proposition, not a minor footnote.

artifactLines: Nina draws on a classic agency lineage: Schwab, Publicis, McCann, and Saatchi & Saatchi.
Wanting to see if it worked, the author fed it a real brand idea to test.
Nina caught something during the intake stage that others would likely miss.
She treated that caught detail as the entire proposition, not a minor footnote.

beats.narration_text: What got me is that Nina caught something in the intake and treated it as the entire proposition, not a detail, evoking that Schwab-Publicis-McCann agency lineage. I want to reverse-engineer her judgment. Help me: (1) feed a brand idea of mine through a similar intake and note what she flags first; (2) compare her framing of the core proposition against my own instinct; (3) test whether that classic ad-agency discipline actually improves my positioning or just sounds authoritative.
